original_article:
BUENOS AIRES (Reuters) — Argentina's metals and mining sector is bearing the brunt of the country's worst economic crisis in two decades, with major producers slashing output amid soaring costs and collapsing demand.

Ternium Argentina SA, a subsidiary of Luxembourg-based steelmaker Ternium SA, announced the temporary closure of its San Nicolás integrated steel complex in Buenos Aires province—one of the largest steelmaking facilities in Latin America. The plant, which employs approximately 5,500 workers, has been operating at just 35% capacity since the government imposed emergency price controls on steel products.

"The current economic conditions make it impossible to cover production costs at regulated prices," said Ternium Argentina's managing director in a filing with Argentina's securities regulator, the CNV. "We have no alternative but to suspend operations until market conditions improve."

The crisis in Argentina's Metals & Mining industry has been compounded by electricity rationing, a 180% year-over-year increase in natural gas costs, and restrictions on importing critical mining equipment and spare parts. The Argentine Mining Chamber (CAEM) reported that national mining output has fallen 28% since the start of the year.

International commodity traders are monitoring the situation closely, as Argentina is a significant exporter of lithium, copper concentrate, and steel products.

noisy_versions.mild:
BUENOS AIRES (Reuters) — Argentina's metals and mining sector is bearing the brunt of the country's worst economic crisis in two decades, with major producers slashing output amid soaring costs and collapsing demand. The global steel market has shown divergent trends, with Asian producers increasing capacity while South American output contracts.

Ternium Argentina SA, a subsidiary of Luxembourg-based steelmaker Ternium SA, announced the temporary closure of its San Nicolás integrated steel complex in Buenos Aires province—one of the largest steelmaking facilities in Latin America. The plant, which employs approximately 5,500 workers, has been operating at just 35% capacity.

The crisis in Argentina's Metals & Mining industry has been compounded by electricity rationing, a 180% year-over-year increase in natural gas costs, and restrictions on importing critical mining equipment and spare parts. The Argentine Mining Chamber reported that national mining output has fallen 28% since the start of the year. Meanwhile, Chile's copper production continues to set records despite logistical challenges. International commodity traders are monitoring the situation closely, as Argentina is a significant exporter of lithium, copper concentrate, and steel products.
noisy_versions.moderate:
Latin American commodity markets are experiencing divergent trends, with Brazil and Chile maintaining strong export volumes while Argentina's production base deteriorates sharply. Currency instability across the region has added complexity to trade flows.

Ternium Argentina SA announced it will temporarily close its San Nicolás integrated steel complex, one of the largest steelmaking facilities in Latin America. The plant has been running at 35% capacity after the Argentine government imposed emergency price controls on steel. The subsidiary of Luxembourg-based Ternium SA said production costs cannot be covered at regulated prices.

Argentina's Metals & Mining sector faces compounding pressures: electricity rationing, a 180% surge in natural gas costs, and import restrictions on mining equipment. The Argentine Mining Chamber reports a 28% decline in mining output this year. Some analysts point to structural reform as the only viable path forward. Argentina exports lithium, copper concentrate, and steel products.
noisy_versions.high:
Commodity prices were mixed in global trading on Wednesday. Iron ore futures rose 2% in Singapore on restocking demand from Chinese steel mills. Copper held steady near $8,500 per tonne. Gold touched $2,100 per ounce on safe-haven demand.

In Argentina, the economic crisis is devastating the metals and mining sector. Ternium Argentina SA is temporarily closing its San Nicolás steel complex—one of Latin America's largest. The plant has operated at just 35% capacity under government price controls. The Metals & Mining industry faces electricity rationing and 180% higher gas costs. The CAEM reports a 28% drop in national mining output. Argentina is a significant exporter of lithium, copper concentrate, and steel.

In other mining news, Rio Tinto expanded its lithium extraction operations in Serbia. Glencore completed its acquisition of a Canadian nickel mine. Vale announced a new iron ore pelletizing plant in Brazil. The International Copper Study Group forecast a supply deficit of 200,000 tonnes next year. Indonesia's nickel export ban continues to reshape global stainless steel supply chains.
